title: Asparagus, Eggs and Mayonnaise
excerpt: This dish is a combination of three recipes – you can use any of the recipes on their own, or together with other dishes. The eggs are special: they're flavoured poached eggs that are foolproof. The mayonnaise is the easiest thing in the world if you have a stick blender, and versatile too, …

seo.title: Asparagus, Eggs and Mayonnaise - Marinda Kook

html:
This dish is a combination of three recipes – you can use any of the recipes on their own, or together with other dishes. The eggs are special: they're flavoured poached eggs that are foolproof. The mayonnaise is the easiest thing in the world if you have a stick blender, and versatile too, because you can add all sorts of flavours.

Ingredients

Mayonnaise

  • 1 Jumbo egg
  • 15 ml Plain white vinegar
  • 250 ml Sunflower oil
  • 2.5 ml Salt
  • 30 ml Dried tarragon

Tip: You can use any oil or combination of oils. I prefer plain sunflower oil. And you can use any flavourings: garlic, lemon zest, basil, curry powder and coriander leaves … the options are endless.

Asparagus

  • 12 – 16 young, green asparagus spears
  • salted cooking water to taste
  • ice water

Eggs

  • 4 small bowls or cups
  • cling wrap
  • 4 jumbo eggs
  • masala powder of your choice
  • dried red chilli flakes
  • finely chopped spring onions
  • coarsely ground salt and pepper

Tip: You can use any flavour combination: smoked paprika and chopped coriander leaves are delicious – or even garlic, chopped parsley and grated Parmesan cheese.

Method

Mayonnaise

Crack the egg into the deep, upright cup of your stick blender.

Then place the rest of the ingredients (except the tarragon) in it in this order: vinegar, then oil, then salt.

Gently push your stick blender in until it touches the bottom of your cup. Press the button so it starts blending and hold it there until the mixture begins to emulsify at the bottom.

While the blender is still switched on, pull it slowly but purposefully upwards – and as it moves upward, you'll see the whole lot become a wonderful, delicious mayonnaise.

Stop immediately once the blender reaches the top of the ingredients.

Stir in the tarragon, close the cup and set aside until needed.

Asparagus

Fill a pot 2/3 full with cooking water and salt the water to taste.

Cook the asparagus quickly (about 90 seconds) in the pot with a lid on until just done. They should still be crisp.

Lift the asparagus out of the cooking water with a slotted spoon and immediately dunk them in the ice water. Keep the cooking water to poach the eggs in.

Masala-poached Eggs

Spray each cup with non-stick spray.

Line the cups with large pieces of cling wrap – enough to tie a knot. Brush the inside of each piece of cling wrap with any oil.

Sprinkle masala powder lightly on the cling wrap – the bottom and sides.

Scatter a few chilli flakes and spring onion pieces on the bottom of each cup.

Carefully crack an egg into each cup.

Sprinkle the tops of each egg lightly with masala, chilli flakes and spring onion pieces and season with salt and pepper.

Bring the corners of each cup's cling wrap together and tie tightly; leave a small gap between the egg and the knot.

Bring the water in which the asparagus was cooked to a gentle rolling boil and poach the eggs in the water for 3 – 6 minutes (depending on their size) until they reach the doneness you like.

Remove the eggs from the water immediately and place them in a colander, still in the plastic.

Serving

Serve immediately once the eggs are ready: dunk the cold asparagus in the water in which the eggs were poached for one minute to warm it up again.

Spread a generous layer of mayonnaise on each serving plate.

Drain the asparagus and arrange about 3 – 4 spears on each plate.

Carefully cut open the plastic on each egg and tip the egg out onto the asparagus.

Spoon a generous portion of mayonnaise on top of each egg and garnish however you like with whatever is edible and flamboyant in your house.

Makes about 300 ml mayonnaise, 4 servings as a starter

A bit of effort, but Easy!
